description_long: This is a classic wooden barrel with a cylindrical, slightly bulging body constructed from vertical wooden staves. The staves are held tightly together by four wide, dark metal hoops that encircle the barrel at the top, middle, and bottom, providing structural reinforcement. The wood appears weathered and aged, with visible grain, knots, and some minor surface imperfections, suggesting long-term outdoor or utilitarian use. The top of the barrel is closed with a flat, circular wooden lid made from several planks, flush with the rim. There are no visible handles, spigots, or decorative markings, giving it a utilitarian and rustic appearance. The overall look is robust and functional, suitable for storage or transport of goods in urban, industrial, or historical settings.
description: A weathered wooden barrel with metal hoops and a flat wooden lid, showing rustic and utilitarian features. The surface is aged, with visible wood grain and no handles or markings.
description_view_0: Front view showing the bulging wooden staves, four metal hoops, and the flat wooden lid with visible wood grain.
description_view_1: Slightly rotated view revealing more of the wood texture and the seamless connection of the metal hoops around the barrel.
description_view_2: Another side view, similar to the others, highlighting the consistent weathered wood and metal banding.
description_view_3: Back view, nearly identical to the front, with the same metal hoops and aged wood, no additional features visible.
